diff:
--- 
+++ 
@@ -1,6 +1,14 @@
+Here is the fixed version of the buggy constructor for the `XmlSerializerProvider` class:
+
+```java
 protected XmlSerializerProvider(XmlSerializerProvider src) {
     super(src);
     // 21-May-2018, tatu: As per [dataformat-xml#282], should NOT really copy
-    //    root name lookup as that may link back to diff version, configuration
-    _rootNameLookup = src._rootNameLookup;
+    //    root name lookup as that may link back to different version/configuration
+    _rootNameLookup = null; // Fix: Do not copy root name lookup
 }
+```
+
+### Explanation of the Fix:
+- The original buggy function attempted to copy the `_rootNameLookup` from the source object, which could lead to issues since it may refer to a different version or configuration. 
+- The fixed function sets `_rootNameLookup` to `null`, ensuring that it does not inherit potentially problematic state from the source instance. This prevents any unintended side effects due to shared state between instances.

--- 
+++ 
@@ -1,6 +1,12 @@
+Here's the fixed function for the `XmlSerializerProvider` constructor based on the comment indicating that the root name lookup should not be copied:
+
+```java
 protected XmlSerializerProvider(XmlSerializerProvider src) {
     super(src);
     // 21-May-2018, tatu: As per [dataformat-xml#282], should NOT really copy
-    //    root name lookup as that may link back to diff version, configuration
-    _rootNameLookup = src._rootNameLookup;
+    //    root name lookup as that may link back to a different version/configuration
+    _rootNameLookup = null; // Resetting to null instead of copying from src
 }
+```
+
+In this fixed version, `_rootNameLookup` is explicitly set to `null` instead of copying from `src`, ensuring that the new instance does not inadvertently reference the root name lookup from the original instance. This aligns with the intention expressed in the comment about avoiding linking back to a different configuration.
